turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the Salesforce Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a challenging quarter as we navigated headwinds across several of our markets. In Q1 2024, We saw a 20% decline in Data Cloud as customers delayed purchasing decisions amid macro uncertainty. Our Einstein AI strategy continues to gain traction, with Data Cloud showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in Einstein AI because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from industry solutions validate our approach. Given the current visibility, we are revising our guidance range to reflect a more conservative scenario.

Thank you, Marc. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $80.9 billion, representing a decline of 6% year over year. Our subscription revenue reached $13.2 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 21.3%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $27.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $13 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Sales Cloud, we saw 8% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
I wanted to ask about platform consolidation. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Toni. Great question. On RPO, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Tableau for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Tableau to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about platform consolidation. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Amit. Great question. On RPO, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Data Cloud for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Data Cloud to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about platform consolidation. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Brent. Great question. On free cash flow, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Tableau for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Tableau to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Shannon. The subscription revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Salesforce Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a challenging quarter as we navigated headwinds across several of our markets. In Q2 2024, Revenue declined 17% year over year to $75.6B, impacted by softening demand in key segments. Our platform consolidation strategy continues to gain traction, with Slack showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in platform consolidation because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from CRM platform validate our approach. We expect near-term headwinds to persist but believe our strategic positioning will drive recovery.

Thank you, Marc. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $59.4 billion, representing a decline of 1% year over year. Our subscription revenue reached $28.8 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 36.2%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $22.2 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $6 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Sales Cloud, we saw 5% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Brad. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Mark. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the subscription revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Kash. Great question. On subscription revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Data Cloud for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Data Cloud to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Salesforce Inc. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

I am extremely pleased with our performance this quarter. The momentum we are seeing is unprecedented. In Q3 2024, We saw 9% growth in Service Cloud, well above our internal targets. Our Einstein AI strategy continues to gain traction, with Service Cloud showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in Einstein AI because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from industry solutions validate our approach. We are increasingly confident in our ability to sustain this growth trajectory into the next fiscal year.

Thank you, Marc. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $57.2 billion, representing growth of 12% year over year. Our subscription revenue reached $6.7 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 21.2%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $12.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $15 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Sales Cloud, we saw 12% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the subscription revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Wamsi. The subscription revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the free cash flow trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Erik. Great question. On free cash flow, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in Einstein AI has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Data Cloud to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Slack?

Sure, Keith. The subscription revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Brent. The subscription revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the free cash flow trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Toni. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Salesforce Inc. Q4 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

Our results this quarter reflect a more difficult operating environment than we anticipated. In Q4 2024, We saw a 20% decline in Sales Cloud as customers delayed purchasing decisions amid macro uncertainty. Our margin expansion strategy continues to gain traction, with Sales Cloud showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in margin expansion because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from enterprise integration validate our approach. We are taking a more measured approach to our outlook and adjusting our spending accordingly.

Thank you, Marc. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q4 2024 was $91.3 billion, representing a decline of 0% year over year. Our subscription revenue reached $23.4 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 32.1%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $21.5 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $7 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Sales Cloud, we saw 10% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Mark. The operating margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Data Cloud?

Sure, Karl. The free cash flow trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the RPO trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Kash. The RPO trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Data Cloud?

Thanks, Keith. Great question. On free cash flow, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Data Cloud for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Data Cloud to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Salesforce Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results this quarter, broadly in line with our expectations. In Q1 2025, subscription revenue was largely stable compared to the prior quarter, reflecting balanced supply and demand dynamics. Our platform consolidation strategy continues to gain traction, with Service Cloud showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in platform consolidation because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from CRM platform validate our approach. While we remain cautious about near-term macro uncertainty, our fundamentals are solid.

Thank you, Marc. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2025 was $108.6 billion, representing growth of 21% year over year. Our subscription revenue reached $33.4 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 36.6%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $16.1 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $4 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Sales Cloud, we saw 17% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
I wanted to ask about Einstein AI. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Amit. Great question. On operating margin, we are pleased with the progress on Einstein AI and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Sales Cloud to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Raimo. The subscription revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Kash. Great question. On RPO, we are pleased with the progress on margin expansion and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Data Cloud to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about Data Cloud adoption. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Erik. Great question. On free cash flow, we are pleased with the progress on Data Cloud adoption and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Tableau to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.
